story.iWant: seed BMAD workflows, agents, and configurations from YAML/Markdown files into the database
story.soThat: the workflow engine has all necessary metadata to execute BMAD workflows on initial setup
story.tasks: - Create workflows seed file (parse BMM/CIS workflow.yaml files, read instructions.md, map agents, determine patterns) - Update seed.ts main script (import workflows seed, call after agents/paths, add error handling) - Test seeding (run bun db:seed, verify agents/paths/workflows seeded, test idempotency)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Seed script reads BMAD files from bmad/ directory
All BMM workflows seeded into workflows table
All CIS workflows seeded into workflows table
6 core agents seeded into agents table (Analyst, PM, Architect, DEV, SM, UX Designer)
Agent capabilities seeded (already in agents table as JSONB)
Workflow paths seeded (greenfield-level-0 through greenfield-level-4, brownfield variants)
Test user seeded into user table (one user for local development)
Seed script is idempotent (can run multiple times safely)

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: Implement seed system that loads BMAD workflows, agents, and configurations from YAML/Markdown files into the database. Parse YAML using js-yaml library, store workflow instructions as text, store YAML config as JSON.

doc.title: Story 1.1 (Previous Story)
doc.snippet: Database schema with 16 tables implemented. Agents table uses JSONB for capabilitiesJson. Workflows table has name, agent_id, pattern, instructions, yamlConfig fields. Seeding infrastructure ready with db:seed script.

doc.title: Root Package Config
doc.snippet: Uses Bun package manager (packageManager: bun@1.3.0). Database commands use turbo to run workspace scripts (db:seed runs turbo -F @chiron/scripts seed).

artifact.reason: Main seed entry point - needs to import and call workflows seed function after agents/paths seeding
Pattern reference for idempotent seeding using onConflictDoNothing(), shows agent data structure
Pattern reference for parsing YAML files, recursive directory scanning, and seeding workflow paths
Workflows table schema - defines name, agentId, pattern, instructions, yamlConfig columns
Agents table schema - shows capabilitiesJson JSONB field structure
Example workflow YAML structure showing name, description, config_source, installed_path, template, instructions fields
Example CIS workflow YAML structure for pattern reference
User table schema - seed one test user using better-auth API

story-context.constraints: - Use Bun as package manager and runtime (not npm/node) - Use Drizzle ORM .onConflictDoNothing() for idempotent seeding (same pattern as agents.ts and workflow-paths.ts) - Recursively scan bmad/ directories for workflow.yaml files (BMM: bmad/bmm/workflows/**, CIS: bmad/cis/workflows/**) - Map workflow names to agents using naming conventions (product-brief→analyst, prd→pm, architecture→architect, dev-story→dev, sprint-planning→sm, create-ux-design→ux-designer) - Determine chat patterns using heuristics (brainstorm/design-thinking→structured, review/validate→focused, planning/sprint→parallel, default→sequential) - Store workflow instructions as text in workflows.instructions column - Store YAML config as JSON in workflows.yamlConfig column - Perform foreign key lookup (get agent.id) before inserting workflows - Skip game-design workflow path (excluded from MVP, same as workflow-paths.ts) - Create workflows seed file at packages/scripts/src/seeds/workflows.ts following same structure as agents.ts and workflow-paths.ts - Seed one test user using better-auth (not direct DB insert) - email: test@chiron.local, name: Test User, password: test123 - Use better-auth's user creation API to ensure proper password hashing and validation
story-context.interfaces: <interface name="seedWorkflows" kind="function" signature="export async function seedWorkflows(): Promise" path="packages/scripts/src/seeds/workflows.ts" />

tests.standards: No formal test framework configured yet for Epic 1. Use manual verification: run bun db:seed, query database to verify record counts, run seed twice to verify no duplicates created.
tests.locations: None yet - testing strategy deferred to later epics

ideas.idea: Query workflows table after seeding, count BMM workflows, verify >20 workflows from bmad/bmm/workflows/**
Query workflows table, count CIS workflows, verify 4 workflows (design-thinking, innovation-strategy, problem-solving, storytelling)
Query agents table, verify 6 core agents exist with correct names (analyst, pm, architect, dev, sm, ux-designer)
Query workflow_paths table, verify 10 paths seeded (5 greenfield, 5 brownfield, excluding game-design)
Run bun db:seed twice consecutively, verify row counts remain same (no duplicates)
Execute bun db:seed from project root, verify exit code 0 and no errors in output
